games.title: Ace of Penguins
games.subtitle: A collection of classic card and puzzle games
games.source_name: Ace of Penguins by DJ Delorie and contributors
games.input: Mouse; keyboard for menus and help
games.summary: Play Freecell, Solitaire, Spider, Minesweeper, Taipei and several other compact classics with a penguin theme.
games.why_play: A relaxed collection for short solo sessions. Freecell is the tested starting game, while the package includes eleven more card and puzzle games.
controls: Move cards
Click or drag cards with the mouse. Build descending alternating-colour columns and move aces to the foundations.
Help
Press F1 for the built-in rules and game help.
Leave
Press Escape or Q to close the current game.
quick_start: Download and run the Linux install-and-play helper.
Freecell opens first. Move cards with the mouse.
Use the Game menu for a new deal; press F1 whenever you need the full rules.
Other included games appear in the Linux application menu under Games.
games.qa: Freecell reached an active board and a card was moved to a foundation with networking disabled.

games.title: TuxMath
games.subtitle: Arcade maths practice with Tux
games.summary: Protect penguin igloos by solving the maths problems carried by falling comets.
games.why_play: It turns arithmetic practice into a quick arcade challenge, with modes ranging from number typing to addition, subtraction and multiplication.
controls: Choose a mode
Use the arrow keys and Enter in the menus.
Answer
Type the answer with the number keys, then press Space or Enter.
Factoroids
In Factoroids, use number keys to steer and enter a valid factor to fire.
quick_start: Download and run the Linux install-and-play helper.
Choose Play Alone, then select an activity and difficulty.
Read the falling problem, type its answer and press Enter.
Keep answering before the comets reach the igloos.
games.qa: Reached Number Typing gameplay, entered an answer and observed the active Wave 1 playfield with networking disabled.

games.title: Sopwith
games.subtitle: Classic side-scrolling biplane combat
games.source_name: SDL Sopwith by David L. Clark, Simon Howard, Jesse Smith and contributors
games.players: 1 player; 2 over LAN with manual setup
games.summary: Fly a First World War biplane, bomb ground targets and dogfight enemy aircraft in a deliberately old-school arcade game.
games.why_play: Simple controls hide a demanding flight model. The helper starts novice mode with infinite ammunition and no stalls, which is the best first flight.
controls: Fly
Comma pulls up, slash pulls down and period flips the plane.
Throttle
Z slows down and X speeds up.
Weapons
Space fires the machine gun and B drops a bomb.
Return home
Press H near your runway to ask the autopilot to land.
quick_start: Download and run the Linux install-and-play helper; it starts novice mode.
Hold X to accelerate along the runway, then use comma and slash to control the nose.
Use Space against aircraft and B while passing over ground targets.
Destroy all enemy targets while keeping enough height to return safely.
games.qa: Reached a live novice flight with aircraft, terrain and HUD visible while networking was disabled.

games.title: Battle Tanks
games.subtitle: Fast cartoon tank battles
games.source_name: Battle Tanks by the Battle Tanks Team
games.license: Battle Tanks open-source licence with LGPL and other bundled components
games.summary: Drive a tank, Shilka or rocket launcher through reactive arenas packed with weapons, vehicles and destructible scenery.
games.why_play: It supports solo battles, same-computer split screen and LAN play. Deathmatch is the quickest way to learn.
controls: Drive
Arrow keys move and steer with the default keyboard layout.
Fire
Ctrl fires the primary weapon; Alt fires the alternate weapon.
Change controls
Open Options from the main menu to inspect or change each player's keys or gamepad.
quick_start: Download and run the Linux install-and-play helper.
Enter a player name, then choose Start Game.
Choose Deathmatch, a vehicle and a map.
Use the arrow keys to drive, Ctrl for the main weapon and Alt for the alternate weapon.
games.qa: Created a player, selected a deathmatch map and reached a live arena with a controllable tank and HUD while networking was disabled.

games.title: Liquid War
games.subtitle: Guide a flowing army with one cursor
games.source_name: Liquid War by Christian Mauduit and contributors
games.license: GPL family licence as packaged by Debian
games.summary: Move a single cursor and watch hundreds of tiny fighters flow around walls, surround opponents and consume rival armies.
games.why_play: The rules are immediate but the movement feels unlike a conventional strategy game. It is especially lively with several local players.
controls: Lead the army
Move your coloured cursor. Your entire swarm continuously tries to reach it.
Fight
Guide your swarm around obstacles and through enemy colours; touching fighters consume one another.
Win
Keep more of your colour alive and surround separated enemy groups.
quick_start: Download and run the Linux install-and-play helper.
Choose Play, then begin with a single-player match.
Move your cursor across the battlefield and let your coloured army follow.
Sweep around walls to surround enemy colours instead of pushing straight through them.
games.qa: Reached an active multicolour battlefield, moved the player cursor and observed the battlefield change while networking was disabled.

games.title: FreedroidRPG
games.subtitle: A story-rich action RPG about rebel robots
games.license: GPL-2+ with GPL-3, Expat, BSD and CC0 components
games.summary: Play as Tux in a ruined future, fight rebel robots, complete quests and take control of enemy machines.
games.why_play: This is the deepest game in the batch, with dialogue, real-time combat, equipment, skills and a campaign designed for many sessions.
controls: Move and attack
Left-click the ground to move. Left-click an enemy to approach and attack.
Run and skills
Hold Ctrl while moving to run. Right-click uses the selected skill or program.
Panels
I opens inventory, C opens character details, S opens skills and Q opens the quest log.
Save
F3 quicksaves and F4 quickloads. Use the main menu for named saves.
quick_start: Download and run the Linux install-and-play helper.
Choose Play, New Hero, enter a name and continue through the opening story.
Follow the first conversation and use its response choices to begin the tutorial.
Left-click to move or attack; press F1 at any time for the full key chart.
games.qa: Created ArcadeQA, entered the opening story and reached interactive dialogue choices while networking was disabled.
